findings.reasoning: This page is dedicated to the TurboTax Full Service 'Beat Your Price' offer, which is a paid, expert-assisted tax preparation service. There is no mention of 'TurboTax Free Edition' or the free tax filing product anywhere in the page content. The footer navigation includes a link labeled 'Free Edition tax filing' pointing to the free-edition.jsp page, but this is a standard site navigation link, not a marketing mention of the free product on this page itself. The phrase '100% free to start. Pay only when we file' refers to the Full Service payment structure (you don't pay until the return is filed), not to TurboTax Free Edition. Since TurboTax Free (the $0 product) is not substantively mentioned/marketed on this page, the trigger for R-01 (disclosure required when 'TurboTax free is mentioned') is not met. The strongest counter-reading is that the footer nav link 'Free Edition tax filing' could count as a 'mention' of TurboTax Free, but this is a generic site-wide navigation menu item present on virtually all TurboTax pages, not a promotional claim about free filing that would need an eligibility disclosure right underneath it. Treating boilerplate footer navigation links as triggering this rule would be an overly broad reading not supported by the rule's intent, which targets marketing mentions of the free product's cost/availability claims.

findings.location: footer navigation link under “Online Software Products”
findings.reasoning: The rule triggers whenever TurboTax Free is mentioned. The footer includes the link text “Free Edition tax filing,” which is a reference to TurboTax’s free product. There is no accompanying disclosure anywhere “right underneath” this mention stating “~37% of filers qualify. Simple Form 1040 returns only (no schedules, except for EITC, CTC, student loan interest, and Schedule 1-A).” Nor is there any paraphrased version of that disclosure elsewhere on the page. Therefore Axis A is not met (required disclosure is absent in the required proximity), and Axis B is also not met because there is no matching or equivalent wording at all. The strongest counterargument is that a simple navigational footer link might be exempt or that the disclosure could be on the linked Free Edition page instead of this blog page; however, the rule text does not provide such an exception and explicitly requires the disclosure on the page where TurboTax Free is mentioned, directly underneath. Thus this is a non-compliant, unapproved violation and must be flagged.

findings.location: shared footer – Online Software Products list
findings.reasoning: Trigger met because the page explicitly mentions the TurboTax Free Edition product via the “Free Edition tax filing” link. Rule R-01 requires the specific eligibility disclosure to appear right underneath such a mention. No such disclosure (~37% of filers qualify. Simple Form 1040 returns only...) appears underneath or near this mention, so Axis A is not compliant. Because there is no disclosure text present, it cannot match the approved library entry, so Axis B is false. Strongest counter-reading: the page’s other uses of “free” (e.g., “Start for Free” and “100% free to start”) might be seen as triggering, but the rule is specifically about the TurboTax Free product, and the required disclosure pertains to Free Edition eligibility, not generic “free to start” messaging. Therefore, only the “Free Edition tax filing” mention triggers the rule, and it lacks the required disclosure.
